item.title: Major fire at Ryazan refinery as Ukraine launches large-scale drone strikes on Russia
item.description: Ukrainian forces carried out a major drone attack on Russian energy infrastructure, with a large fire erupting at the Ryazan oil refinery. Local reports indicate casualties and damage to residential buildings from drone debris.

item.title: India PM Modi begins five-nation tour seeking energy stability amid Iran war disruptions
item.description: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi started a diplomatic tour to UAE, Netherlands, Sweden, Norway and Italy to secure energy supplies and strengthen trade ties following the India-EU free trade agreement. The trip underscores India's efforts to diversify partnerships amid Middle East volatility.

item.title: China renews US beef import licenses during Trump-Xi summit, alarming Brazilian exporters
item.description: Beijing renewed import licenses for hundreds of US beef plants during the Trump-Xi summit, a reversal from months of frozen trade. Brazil expressed concern as China is its largest beef market.

item.title: Anthropic calls for tighter AI chip export controls as Nvidia CEO joins Trump in Beijing
item.description: AI company Anthropic released a paper urging the US and allies to maintain chip export restrictions to prevent authoritarian governments from gaining AI leadership, timing the release during US-China summit talks.

item.title: Macron acknowledges France outpaced in Africa by China, Turkey and US
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron acknowledged that France has lost competitive ground in Africa to China, Turkey and the United States, as European influence on the continent continues to decline.

item.title: Trump warns patience with Iran running thin as diplomacy presents options
item.description: President Trump said he won't be much more patient with Iran following talks with Xi Jinping, suggesting Tehran could make a deal or face annihilation as diplomatic options remain on the table.

item.title: Solomon Islands elects former China critic Matthew Wale as prime minister
item.description: Solomon Islands lawmakers elected opposition leader Matthew Wale as prime minister, a figure known for questioning the previous government's close ties with Beijing in a shift that could affect Pacific regional dynamics.

item.title: China wants Strait of Hormuz open without restrictions, USTR Greer says
item.description: US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer said China wants the Strait of Hormuz reopened without curbs or tolls, expressing confidence Beijing would act to limit material support for Iran.

item.title: US Justice Department set to drop criminal fraud case against India's Gautam Adani
item.description: The US Justice Department is preparing to drop criminal fraud charges against Indian billionaire Gautam Adani following a settlement agreement, sources said.

item.title: IMF welcomes constructive US-China dialogue as tensions ease
item.description: The IMF said constructive dialogue between the US and China has reduced tensions and benefits the world economy, noting reduced tariff threats and improved cooperation signals.

item.title: Latvian Prime Minister resigns over Ukrainian drone incident handling
item.description: Latvia's Prime Minister Evika Silina resigned, triggering coalition collapse months before October elections, following criticism over handling of Ukrainian drone incidents.

item.title: US expects double-digit billions in Chinese farm purchases after Trump-Xi summit
item.description: The US expects China to make agricultural purchases worth tens of billions following the Trump-Xi summit, as both countries seek to reduce trade tensions.

item.title: IMF team to visit Ukraine for $8.1 billion loan program review
item.description: IMF staff will visit Ukraine in coming weeks to review the country's progress on economic reforms and tax base broadening under its $8.1 billion loan program.

item.title: Gazprom Astrakhan gas plant halts output after drone attack
item.description: Gazprom's gas processing plant in Astrakhan halted motor fuel production after a fire from a May 13 drone attack, industry sources said.

item.title: Taiwan thanks US for support ahead of further Trump-Xi talks
item.description: Taiwan expressed gratitude for continued US support as Beijing made Taiwan a central focus of the Trump-Xi summit discussions.

item.title: Israel pushing for return to Iran war, weighing options with US
item.description: Israel is reportedly pushing for renewed military action against Iran and weighing options with US coordination, according to regional media reports.

item.title: Solomon Islands elects China-cautious opposition leader Matthew Wale as PM
item.description: Solomon Islands lawmakers selected opposition leader Matthew Wale as prime minister, signaling a potential shift away from the previous government's close China alignment.

item.title: Trump blames Biden for US decline after Xi comments
item.description: President Trump dismissed Xi Jinping's comments about US decline and blamed the Biden administration for any perceived weakening of American standing.

item.title: Putin escalating war, not seeking end, says German Chancellor Merz
item.description: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said Putin is escalating rather than seeking to end the Ukraine war, rejecting Russian cease-fire proposals as insufficient.

item.title: Russia losing ground in Ukraine for first time since Kursk incursion
item.description: Russia's military momentum in Ukraine has stalled and shifted, with Ukrainian forces making gains for the first time since the Kursk operation began.

item.title: Saudi Arabia and UAE carried out secret attacks inside Iran
item.description: Saudi Arabia and the UAE secretly conducted operations targeting Iranian facilities during the ongoing conflict, according to intelligence sources.

item.title: Japan wholesale inflation hits 3-year high as Iran war drives import costs
item.description: Japan's wholesale inflation accelerated to a three-year high in April as rising import costs from Middle East disruptions pushed producer prices up.

item.title: China renews licenses for hundreds of US beef exporters amid Trump-Xi summit
item.description: China renewed import licenses for hundreds of US beef exporters during the Trump-Xi summit, signaling a potential thaw in agricultural trade tensions.

item.title: Morocco adds $2B to budget to cushion Middle East conflict impact
item.description: Morocco allocated an additional $2 billion to its budget to offset economic impacts from the Middle East conflict and regional instability.

item.title: Iraq seeks IMF and World Bank financial assistance amid Iran war
item.description: Iraq has sought financial assistance from the IMF and World Bank as economic pressure from the Iran conflict strains its economy and budget.
